data.author: fatpie68
data.text:
$ALP is supposedly buying a 60% Controlling Stake in $GMEE.X from Animoca Brands. Right now, Animoca is in exclusive talks to merge w/ Currenc Group - a FinTech company. 
 
$CURR is a crap stock w/ insiders trying to keep the share price down for the pending merger. If this merger happens, it could be good for Alpha Compute b/c Animoca will control the new FinTech and it's realistic to think they'd use ALP for those Confidential AI Compute needs too. 
 
"Under the terms of the Proposed Transaction, shareholders of Animoca Brands would collectively own approximately 95% of the outstanding shares of the merged entity, with existing $CURR shareholders retaining the remaining 5%. The resulting entity is expected to operate under the Animoca Brands name. Closing is targeted for the third quarter of 2026, with a long stop date of December 31, 2026, which may be extended by an extra 6 months upon mutual agreement." 
 
https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/05/06/3288764/0/en/currenc-group-announces-extension-of-exclusivity-period-for-proposed-reverse-merger-with-animoca-brands-corporation-limited.html

data.author: secretcasino
data.text:
$SDOT Let’s use some common sense here.  
 
Chagay Ravid’s previous firm handled over $10 BILLION in M&A volume. His fund was in Mobileye before Intel bought them for $15 BILLION. He just guided $CURR through a massive Nasdaq SPAC merger last year.  
 
Do you honestly think a guy with a $15B notch on his belt hopped into a dying restaurant shell just to report an $82M loss and nuke his own reputation?  
 
He’s playing every shadow-market game in the book right now and clearly doesn't give a single fuck about the legacy optics or compliance. He is burning the shell to overwrite the ticker. I’m a firm believer that something really, really big is dropping soon. The trap is set...

data.author: Currenc_Group
data.text:
Hong Kong has issued its first batch of stablecoin issuer licenses, marking a major milestone in the development of a regulated digital asset ecosystem. 
 
Anchorpoint, backed by Animoca Brands, Standard Chartered, and HKT, is among the licensed entities under this framework. 
 
Currenc Group (NASDAQ: $CURR ), which has previously announced a strategic alignment with Animoca Brands, is well positioned within the broader digital asset ecosystem as Hong Kong accelerates its regulated Web3 infrastructure. 
 
https://www.tradingview.com/news/cointelegraph:efe636621094b:0-hong-kong-grants-first-stablecoin-licenses-to-anchorpoint-and-hsbc/

data.author: secretcasino
data.text:
@glorrel if you take a look at $CURR (which is $SDOT's CEO Ravid's previous company) you can see they are about to be sitting on $400 million in cash. CURR is up 25% over the last two days. 
 
SDOT's current contracted CFO is also CEO of Millennium Food Tech, an Israeli R&D partnership company that is looking to spin off a few of its portfolio companies. Aleph Farms (one of the companies that is in Millennium Food's portfolio) would be my guess for an M+A situation here. Alpeh also just announced a new Singapore hub a few days ago. Sadot also has an office in Singapore. Sadot needs cash right now due to an Accounts receivable issue last quarter. I could see Ravid fixing this issue with a merger and an influx of liquid capital.

data.author: secretcasino
data.text:
@Cal1f0rn1aBay Would you rather own 100% of a bicycle ($4M grain company) or 3% of a Ferrari ($100M+ global food-tech leader with a $400M cash backstop)? 
 
Even if millions of new shares are issued to Aleph's owners, they will be Restricted/Locked for 6–12 months. 
 
The public trading float stays at 1.4 million shares. 
 
If 121,000 shorts (paying 93% interest) have to cover into a 1.4M float when the $400M cash news ($CURR) hits, the 'percentage ownership' doesn't determine the price, the supply shock does. 
 
Again, this is all speculation on my end, but i think there's too many business connections, two non-compliant companies that need to let Nasdaq know what's going on, and ultimately we are being met with radio silence through this quiet period while they figure everything out.  
 
I think we could be looking at a $30 per share price here once we factor in the Millennium Food Tech/Aleph value IMO.

source_row.text_snippet:
@Cal1f0rn1aBay Would you rather own 100% of a bicycle ($4M grain company) or 3% of a Ferrari ($100M+ global food-tech leader with a $400M cash backstop)? 
 
Even if millions of new shares are issued to Aleph's owners, they will be Restricted/Locked for 6–12 months. 
 
The public trading float stays at 1.4 million shares. 
 
If 121,000 shorts (paying 93% interest) have to cover into a 1.4M float when the $400M cash news ($CURR) hits, the 'percentage ownership' doesn't determine the price, the supply shock does. 
 
Again, this is all speculation on my end, but i think there's too many business connections, two non-compliant companies that need to let Nasdaq know what's going on, and ultimately we are being met with radio silence through this quiet period while they figure everything out.  
 
I think we could be looking at a $30 per share price here once we factor in the Millennium Food Tech/Aleph value IMO.

data.author: secretcasino
data.text:
$SDOT ok i finally think i know what's going on here... 
 
Look at Ravid’s history as CFO of Seamless Group ($CURR). He got $CURR  a Nasdaq listing by using the exact same meeting delay tactics. 
 
While we wait, Oren Attiya (Sadot's "rent-a-CFO"  & Millennium Food-Tech CEO) is likely wiring the pipes for a backdoor merger.  
 
He just sold Pokemoto at $SDOT. He’s clearing the "old junk" out of the shell to make room for the Millennium/Aleph Farms portfolio.  
 
The "No Meeting" news isn't a red flag—it’s a blackout period. They don't want a meeting until they can announce the Millennium merger and a new ticker identity. 🚀 
 
Shorts think they’re winning the delisting bet. I think they’re walking into a trap set by the same guy who just pulled this off at $CURR. 🤡🪤 
 
all hypothetical of course, but the connections are too eerie...
